74	// reVerdictLine matches "### Verdict: APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|BLOCK"
75	// anywhere in the output, case-insensitive, allowing 1–4 leading hashes.
76	var reVerdictLine = regexp.MustCompile(`(?im)^#{1,4}\s+Verdict:\s*(APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|BLOCK)\s*$`)
77	var reReviewSectionHeading = regexp.MustCompile(`(?im)^#{1,4}\s+(AC Grades|Summary|Findings)\s*:?\s*$`)
78	var reACReference = regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)\bAC#\s*([0-9]+)\b`)
79	
80	// ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable is returned by ParseReviewVerdictStrict when
81	// the reviewer output does not contain a recognizable `### Verdict: ...`
82	// line. Callers should surface this as a review-error (retryable) rather
83	// than silently treating it as BLOCK — the pre-ddx-f7ae036f behavior was to
84	// default-to-BLOCK, which produced spurious APPROVE→BLOCK mis-records
85	// whenever the reviewer's output shape drifted (codex stream frames,
86	// rationale-only responses, stall-truncated output).
87	var ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable = fmt.Errorf("reviewer output: no recognizable verdict line")
88	
89	// ParseReviewVerdictStrict is the structured-error variant of
90	// ParseReviewVerdict: on unparseable input it returns
91	// ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable instead of silently returning VerdictBlock.
92	// The execute-loop uses this path so a parse failure reopens the bead for
93	// retry rather than recording a false BLOCK.
94	func ParseReviewVerdictStrict(output string) (ReviewVerdict, error) {
95		m := reVerdictLine.FindStringSubmatch(output)
96		if m == nil {
97			return "", ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable
98		}
99		switch strings.ToUpper(strings.TrimSpace(m[1])) {
100		case "APPROVE":
101			return VerdictApprove, nil
102		case "REQUEST_CHANGES":
103			return VerdictRequestChanges, nil
104		case "BLOCK":
105			return VerdictBlock, nil
106		default:
107			return "", ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable
108		}
109	}
110	
111	// ParseReviewVerdict extracts the verdict from a review agent's output.
112	// The expected format includes a line: ### Verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | BLOCK
113	//
114	// Deprecated: returns VerdictBlock on unparseable input, which masks
115	// legitimate verdict-parse failures as BLOCKs and caused the wave-2
116	// review-malfunction incident (ddx-f7ae036f). New callers should use
117	// ParseReviewVerdictStrict and treat ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable as a
118	// retryable review-error.
119	func ParseReviewVerdict(output string) ReviewVerdict {
120		v, err := ParseReviewVerdictStrict(output)
121		if err != nil {
122			return VerdictBlock
123		}
124		return v
125	}

127	func ParseReviewResult(output string) (ReviewVerdict, []ReviewAC, string) {
128		verdict := ParseReviewVerdict(output)
129		perAC := parseReviewACTable(output)
130		rationale := strings.TrimSpace(extractReviewRationale(output, perAC))
131		if len(perAC) == 0 {
132			perAC = synthesizeReviewACsFromRationale(verdict, rationale)
133		}
134		return verdict, perAC, rationale
135	}

137	func parseReviewACTable(output string) []ReviewAC {
138		lines := strings.Split(output, "\n")
139		var rows []ReviewAC
140		inTable := false
141		for _, raw := range lines {
142			line := strings.TrimSpace(raw)
143			if line == "" {
144				if inTable {
145					break
146				}
147				continue
148			}
149			if reReviewSectionHeading.MatchString(line) {
150				inTable = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(line), "ac grades")
151				continue
152			}
153			if !inTable || !strings.HasPrefix(line, "|") {
154				continue
155			}
156			cols := splitMarkdownTableRow(line)
157			if len(cols) < 4 {
158				continue
159			}
160			if cols[0] == "#" || strings.HasPrefix(cols[0], "---") {
161				continue
162			}
163			n, err := strconv.Atoi(cols[0])
164			if err != nil {
165				continue
166			}
167			rows = append(rows, ReviewAC{
168				Number:   n,
169				Item:     cols[1],
170				Grade:    cols[2],
171				Evidence: cols[3],
172			})
173		}
174		return rows
175	}
176	
177	func splitMarkdownTableRow(line string) []string {
178		parts := strings.Split(line, "|")
179		out := make([]string, 0, len(parts))
180		for _, part := range parts {
181			part = strings.TrimSpace(part)
182			if part == "" {
183				continue
184			}
185			out = append(out, part)
186		}
187		return out
188	}

190	func extractReviewRationale(output string, perAC []ReviewAC) string {
191		lines := strings.Split(output, "\n")
192		var summaryLines []string
193		var findingLines []string
194		var section string
195		for _, raw := range lines {
196			line := strings.TrimSpace(raw)
197			if line == "" {
198				continue
199			}
200			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "## Review:") || strings.HasPrefix(line, "### Verdict:") || strings.HasPrefix(line, "#### Verdict:") {
201				continue
202			}
203			if reReviewSectionHeading.MatchString(line) {
204				heading := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimSuffix(strings.TrimLeft(line, "# "), ":")))
205				switch heading {
206				case "summary":
207					section = "summary"
208				case "findings":
209					section = "findings"
210				case "ac grades":
211					section = "ac"
212				default:
213					section = ""
214				}
215				continue
216			}
217			if section == "ac" && strings.HasPrefix(line, "|") {
218				continue
219			}
220			switch section {
221			case "findings":
222				line = strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(line, "-"))
223				if line != "" {
224					findingLines = append(findingLines, line)
225				}
226			case "summary":
227				summaryLines = append(summaryLines, line)
228			}
229		}
230		if len(findingLines) > 0 {
231			return strings.Join(findingLines, "\n")
232		}
233		if len(summaryLines) > 0 {
234			return strings.Join(summaryLines, "\n")
235		}
236		var unmet []string
237		for _, ac := range perAC {
238			if strings.EqualFold(ac.Grade, string(VerdictApprove)) {
239				continue
240			}
241			label := fmt.Sprintf("AC#%d", ac.Number)
242			if ac.Item != "" {
243				label += " " + ac.Item
244			}
245			if ac.Evidence != "" {
246				unmet = append(unmet, label+": "+ac.Evidence)
247			} else {
248				unmet = append(unmet, label)
249			}
250		}
251		return strings.Join(unmet, "\n")
252	}
253	
254	func synthesizeReviewACsFromRationale(verdict ReviewVerdict, rationale string) []ReviewAC {
255		if rationale == "" {
256			return nil
257		}
258		matches := reACReference.FindAllStringSubmatch(rationale, -1)
259		if len(matches) == 0 {
260			return nil
261		}
262		seen := make(map[int]struct{}, len(matches))
263		out := make([]ReviewAC, 0, len(matches))
264		for _, match := range matches {
265			if len(match) < 2 {
266				continue
267			}
268			n, err := strconv.Atoi(match[1])
269			if err != nil {
270				continue
271			}
272			if _, ok := seen[n]; ok {
273				continue
274			}
275			seen[n] = struct{}{}
276			out = append(out, ReviewAC{
277				Number:   n,
278				Grade:    string(verdict),
279				Evidence: rationale,
280			})
281		}
282		return out
283	}

319	// beadReviewInstructions is the review contract embedded in the prompt.
320	// The reviewing agent must produce APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES / BLOCK with
321	// the exact markdown format described below.
322	const beadReviewInstructions = `You are reviewing a bead implementation against its acceptance criteria.
323	
324	## Your task
325	
326	Examine the diff and each acceptance-criteria (AC) item. For each item assign one grade:
327	
328	- **APPROVE** — fully and correctly implemented; cite the specific file path and line that proves it.
329	- **REQUEST_CHANGES** — partially implemented or has fixable minor issues.
330	- **BLOCK** — not implemented, incorrectly implemented, or the diff is insufficient to evaluate.
331	
332	Overall verdict rule:
333	- All items APPROVE → **APPROVE**
334	- Any item BLOCK → **BLOCK**
335	- Otherwise → **REQUEST_CHANGES**
336	
337	## Required output format
338	
339	Respond with a structured review using exactly this layout (replace placeholder text):
340	
341	---
342	## Review: <bead-id> iter <N>
343	
344	### Verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | BLOCK
345	
346	### AC Grades
347	
348	| # | Item | Grade | Evidence |
349	|---|------|-------|----------|
350	| 1 | <AC item text, max 60 chars> | APPROVE | path/to/file.go:42 — brief note |
351	| 2 | <AC item text, max 60 chars> | BLOCK   | — not found in diff |
352	
353	### Summary
354	
355	<1–3 sentences on overall implementation quality and any recurring theme in findings.>
356	
357	### Findings
358	
359	<Bullet list of REQUEST_CHANGES and BLOCK findings. Each finding must name the specific file, function, or test that is missing or wrong — specific enough for the next agent to act on without re-reading the entire diff. Omit this section entirely if verdict is APPROVE.>`
360	
361	// BuildReviewPrompt builds the complete review prompt for a bead implementation.
362	// It renders the bead metadata, governing document contents, git diff, and
363	// review instructions into an XML-structured prompt string.
364	func BuildReviewPrompt(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
365		var sb strings.Builder
366	
367		sb.WriteString("<bead-review>\n")
368	
369		// ── Bead section ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
370		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
371		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
372	
373		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
374			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", reviewXMLEscape(desc))
375		} else {
376			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
377		}
378	
379		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
380			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", reviewXMLEscape(acc))
381		} else {
382			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
383		}
384	
385		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
386			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
387		}
388	
389		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n\n")
390	
391		// ── Governing docs section ───────────────────────────────────────────────
392		sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
393		if len(refs) == 0 {
394			sb.WriteString("    <note>No governing documents found. Evaluate the diff against the acceptance criteria alone.</note>\n")
395		} else {
396			for _, ref := range refs {
397				if ref.Title != "" {
398					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q title=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path, ref.Title)
399				} else {
400					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path)
401				}
402				docPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(ref.Path))
403				content, readErr := os.ReadFile(docPath)
404				if readErr != nil {
405					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <note>Could not read %s: %s</note>\n", ref.Path, readErr)
406				} else {
407					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <content>\n%s\n      </content>\n", strings.TrimSpace(string(content)))
408				}
409				sb.WriteString("    </ref>\n")
410			}
411		}
412		sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n\n")
413	
414		// ── Diff section ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
415		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <diff rev=%q>\n%s\n  </diff>\n\n", rev, strings.TrimRight(diff, "\n"))
416	
417		// ── Instructions section ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
418		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(beadReviewInstructions, "<bead-id>", b.ID)
419		instructions = strings.ReplaceAll(instructions, "<N>", fmt.Sprintf("%d", iter))
420		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", reviewXMLEscape(instructions))
421	
422		sb.WriteString("</bead-review>\n")
423	
424		return sb.String()
425	}
426	
427	// reviewXMLEscape escapes &, <, and > for inclusion in XML text content.
428	func reviewXMLEscape(s string) string {
429		var buf bytes.Buffer
430		buf.WriteString(strings.NewReplacer(
431			"&", "&",
432			"<", "<",
433			">", ">",
434		).Replace(s))
435		return buf.String()
436	}

438	// DefaultBeadReviewer implements BeadReviewer by dispatching the review
439	// invocation through the agent service (or a test-injected runner).
440	// It fetches the bead, builds the review prompt, and runs the reviewer agent.
441	type DefaultBeadReviewer struct {
442		ProjectRoot string
443		BeadStore   BeadReader
444		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.DdxAgent used to dispatch the
445		// review invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ReviewBead
446		// constructs a fresh service from ProjectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.
447		Service agentlib.DdxAgent
448		// Runner, when non-nil, replaces the service-based dispatch path. Used by
449		// tests to return canned *Result values without spinning up a real
450		// service. Takes precedence over Service.
451		Runner AgentRunner
452		// Harness and Model override the reviewer harness/model.
453		// When empty, Harness defaults to "claude" and Model is resolved
454		// from TierSmart for the chosen harness.
455		Harness string
456		Model   string
457	}

459	// ReviewBead implements BeadReviewer.
460	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) ReviewBead(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev, implHarness, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
461		b, err := r.BeadStore.Get(beadID)
462		if err != nil {
463			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: get bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
464		}
465	
466		// Fetch the git diff for the commit being reviewed.
467		diff, err := r.gitShow(resultRev)
468		if err != nil {
469			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: git show %s: %w", resultRev, err)
470		}
471	
472		// Resolve governing document references.
473		refs := ResolveGoverningRefs(r.ProjectRoot, b)
474	
475		// Determine iteration number from bead events.
476		iter := 1
477	
478		// Build the review prompt.
479		prompt := BuildReviewPrompt(b, iter, resultRev, diff, r.ProjectRoot, refs)
480		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
481		artifacts, err := createArtifactBundle(r.ProjectRoot, r.ProjectRoot, attemptID)
482		if err != nil {
483			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: create artifact bundle: %w", err)
484		}
485		if err := os.WriteFile(artifacts.PromptAbs, []byte(prompt), 0o644); err != nil {
486			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: write prompt artifact: %w", err)
487		}
488	
489		// Resolve reviewer harness and model.
490		reviewHarness := r.Harness
491		if reviewHarness == "" {
492			if implHarness != "" {
493				reviewHarness = implHarness
494			} else {
495				reviewHarness = "claude" // default reviewer harness
496			}
497		}
498		reviewModel := r.Model
499		if reviewModel == "" {
500			reviewModel = ResolveModelTier(reviewHarness, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierSmart))
501		}
502	
503		start := time.Now()
504		runOpts := RunOptions{
505			Context: ctx,
506			Harness: reviewHarness,
507			Model:   reviewModel,
508			Prompt:  prompt,
509			WorkDir: r.ProjectRoot,
510		}
511		result, runErr := r.dispatchReviewRun(ctx, runOpts)
512	
513		durationMS := int(time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
514		if runErr != nil {
515			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
516				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
517				Rationale:       runErr.Error(),
518				Error:           runErr.Error(),
519				ReviewerHarness: reviewHarness,
520				ReviewerModel:   reviewModel,
521				BaseRev:         resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev),
522				ResultRev:       resultRev,
523				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
524				DurationMS:      durationMS,
525			}
526			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
527				Harness:      reviewHarness,
528				Model:        reviewModel,
529				BaseRev:      reviewRes.BaseRev,
530				ResultRev:    resultRev,
531				Verdict:      string(reviewRes.Verdict),
532				BeadID:       beadID,
533				ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
534			}, reviewArtifactResult{
535				Verdict:   string(reviewRes.Verdict),
536				Rationale: reviewRes.Rationale,
537				Error:     reviewRes.Error,
538			})
539			return reviewRes, nil
540		}
541	
542		actualHarness := reviewHarness
543		actualModel := reviewModel
544		if result != nil {
545			if result.Harness != "" {
546				actualHarness = result.Harness
547			}
548			if result.Model != "" {
549				actualModel = result.Model
550			}
551			durationMS = result.DurationMS
552		}
553	
554		output := ""
555		sessionID := ""
556		if result != nil {
557			output = result.Output
558			sessionID = result.AgentSessionID
559		}
560		// Strict parse: unparseable reviewer output surfaces as a typed error so
561		// the execute-loop can classify it as review-error (retryable) rather
562		// than mis-recording it as BLOCK (ddx-f7ae036f). The review result
563		// artifacts still land on disk for forensics via writeReviewArtifacts
564		// below, regardless of the error path.
565		strictVerdict, parseErr := ParseReviewVerdictStrict(output)
566		perAC := parseReviewACTable(output)
567		rationale := strings.TrimSpace(extractReviewRationale(output, perAC))
568		if len(perAC) == 0 {
569			perAC = synthesizeReviewACsFromRationale(strictVerdict, rationale)
570		}
571		baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
572		reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
573			Verdict:         strictVerdict,
574			Rationale:       rationale,
575			PerAC:           perAC,
576			RawOutput:       output,
577			ReviewerHarness: actualHarness,
578			ReviewerModel:   actualModel,
579			SessionID:       sessionID,
580			BaseRev:         baseRev,
581			ResultRev:       resultRev,
582			ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
583			DurationMS:      durationMS,
584		}
585		_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
586			Harness:      actualHarness,
587			Model:        actualModel,
588			SessionID:    sessionID,
589			BaseRev:      baseRev,
590			ResultRev:    resultRev,
591			Verdict:      string(strictVerdict),
592			BeadID:       beadID,
593			ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
594		}, reviewArtifactResult{
595			Verdict:   string(strictVerdict),
596			PerAC:     perAC,
597			Rationale: rationale,
598			Error:     reviewRes.Error,
599		})
600		if parseErr != nil {
601			// Return the review result alongside the parse error so the caller
602			// can surface the full rationale + raw output as review-error event
603			// body (loop's reviewErr path) while still having access to the
604			// reviewer text for operator forensics.
605			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %w (raw output %d bytes; see %s)", parseErr, len(output), artifacts.DirRel)
606		}
607		return reviewRes, nil
608	}

610	// dispatchReviewRun resolves how the review invocation should be executed.
611	// Resolution order matches dispatchAgentRun:
612	//  1. r.Runner (test injection seam) — used directly via Runner.Run.
613	//  2. r.Service (pre-built service) — used via RunViaServiceWith.
614	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(ProjectRoot)
615	//     and dispatch via RunViaServiceWith.
616	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) dispatchReviewRun(ctx context.Context, runOpts RunOptions) (*Result, error) {
617		if r.Runner != nil {
618			return r.Runner.Run(runOpts)
619		}
620		svc := r.Service
621		if svc == nil {
622			built, err := NewServiceFromWorkDir(r.ProjectRoot)
623			if err != nil {
624				return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: build agent service: %w", err)
625			}
626			svc = built
627		}
628		return RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, r.ProjectRoot, runOpts)
629	}

631	// gitShow runs `git show <rev>` with pathspec exclusions for execution-
632	// evidence noise so the review prompt's <diff> section stays bounded even
633	// when an old commit tracked a multi-thousand-line session log. See
634	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs and ddx-39e27896 for the regression.
635	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) gitShow(rev string) (string, error) {
636		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
637		defer cancel()
638		args := append([]string{"show", rev, "--", "."}, EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
639		out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, args...).Output()
640		if err != nil {
641			return "", err
642		}
643		return string(out), nil
644	}

649		}

651		if err != nil {
652			return ""
653		}
654		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
655	}

660		}

662			return err

3	// ClampOutput trims s to at most max bytes. When trimming occurs the
4	// returned text ends with TruncationMarker (the marker is included in
5	// the returned bytes; the length of the returned string may exceed max
6	// only by the marker length when max is too small to fit even the
7	// marker, in which case ClampOutput returns the bare marker truncated
8	// to max bytes). originalBytes is len(s) before clamping.
9	//
10	// FEAT-022 §1 (clamped text output, canonical truncation marker).
11	func ClampOutput(s string, max int) (clamped string, truncated bool, originalBytes int) {
12		originalBytes = len(s)
13		if max < 0 {
14			max = 0
15		}
16		if originalBytes <= max {
17			return s, false, originalBytes
18		}
19		// Reserve room for the marker. If max is smaller than the marker,
20		// return a marker-only string truncated to max bytes (still flagged
21		// truncated so the caller never silently drops content).
22		if max <= len(TruncationMarker) {
23			return TruncationMarker[:max], true, originalBytes
24		}
25		keep := max - len(TruncationMarker)
26		return s[:keep] + TruncationMarker, true, originalBytes
27	}

2	
3	import (
4		"errors"
5		"fmt"
6		"io"
7		"os"
8	)
9	
10	// ErrOversize is returned by hard-fail readers (e.g. user-supplied prompt
11	// files, FEAT-022 §8). Callers compare with errors.Is to surface a
12	// distinct error class to the user.
13	var ErrOversize = errors.New("evidence: input exceeds cap")
14	
15	// OversizeError carries the input-source name, observed size, and cap
16	// for actionable error messages (FEAT-022 Non-Functional / Failure mode
17	// clarity).
18	type OversizeError struct {
19		Source        string
20		ObservedBytes int64
21		CapBytes      int
22	}
23	
24	func (e *OversizeError) Error() string {
25		return fmt.Sprintf("evidence: %s exceeds cap: observed %d bytes, cap %d bytes",
26			e.Source, e.ObservedBytes, e.CapBytes)
27	}
28	
29	func (e *OversizeError) Unwrap() error { return ErrOversize }
30	
31	// ReadFileClamped reads up to max+1 bytes from path. If the file fits
32	// within max it returns (content, false, len(content), nil). If the file
33	// exceeds max it returns the first max bytes plus truncated=true and
34	// originalBytes set to the on-disk size (as observed via os.Stat). The
35	// implementation does not fully load oversize files into memory
36	// (FEAT-022 Non-Functional / Performance).
37	func ReadFileClamped(path string, max int) (content []byte, truncated bool, originalBytes int64, err error) {
38		if max < 0 {
39			return nil, false, 0, fmt.Errorf("evidence: negative cap %d", max)
40		}
41		// nolint:gosec // path is supplied by callers that have already validated it.
42		f, err := os.Open(path)
43		if err != nil {
44			return nil, false, 0, err
45		}
46		defer f.Close()
47	
48		st, err := f.Stat()
49		if err != nil {
50			return nil, false, 0, err
51		}
52		originalBytes = st.Size()
53	
54		// Read max+1 bytes so we can detect overflow without loading the whole file.
55		buf := make([]byte, max+1)
56		n, readErr := io.ReadFull(f, buf)
57		if readErr != nil && readErr != io.EOF && readErr != io.ErrUnexpectedEOF {
58			return nil, false, originalBytes, readErr
59		}
60	
61		if n > max {
62			return buf[:max], true, originalBytes, nil
63		}
64		return buf[:n], false, int64(n), nil
65	}
66	
67	// ReadFileHardFail reads path and returns *OversizeError (wrapping
68	// ErrOversize) if the file exceeds max. Used for user-supplied prompt
69	// files where silent truncation is forbidden (FEAT-022 §8).
70	func ReadFileHardFail(path string, max int, source string) ([]byte, error) {
71		content, truncated, originalBytes, err := ReadFileClamped(path, max)
72		if err != nil {
73			return nil, err
74		}
75		if truncated {
76			return nil, &OversizeError{
77				Source:        source,
78				ObservedBytes: originalBytes,
79				CapBytes:      max,
80			}
81		}
82		return content, nil
83	}

260	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, opts ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
261		if w.Store == nil {
262			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
263		}
264		if w.Executor == nil {
265			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
266		}
267	
268		now := w.Now
269		if now == nil {
270			now = time.Now
271		}
272		assignee := opts.Assignee
273		if assignee == "" {
274			assignee = "ddx"
275		}
276		noProgressCooldown := opts.NoProgressCooldown
277		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
278			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
279		}
280		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := opts.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose
281		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
282			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
283		}
284		heartbeatInterval := opts.HeartbeatInterval
285		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
286			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
287		}
288	
289		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
290		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
291	
292		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
293			writeLoopEvent(opts.EventSink, opts.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
294		}
295	
296		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
297			"worker_id":    opts.WorkerID,
298			"project_root": opts.ProjectRoot,
299			"harness":      opts.Harness,
300			"model":        opts.Model,
301			"session_id":   opts.SessionID,
302			"assignee":     assignee,
303			"once":         opts.Once,
304		})
305		defer func() {
306			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
307				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
308				"successes":           result.Successes,
309				"failures":            result.Failures,
310				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
311			})
312		}()
313	
314		for {
315			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
316			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
317			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
318			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
319			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
320				return result, err
321			}
322	
323			candidate, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, opts.LabelFilter)
324			if err != nil {
325				return result, err
326			}
327			if !ok {
328				if result.Attempts == 0 {
329					result.NoReadyWork = true
330					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
331						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
332							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
333								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
334								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
335								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
336								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
337								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
338							}
339						}
340					}
341				}
342				if opts.PollInterval <= 0 {
343					return result, nil
344				}
345				if err := sleepWithContext(ctx, opts.PollInterval); err != nil {
346					return result, err
347				}
348				continue
349			}
350	
351			attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
352	
353			// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
354			// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
355			// continues (ctx is not cancelled).
356			if opts.PreClaimHook != nil {
357				if hookErr := opts.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
358					if opts.Log != nil {
359						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "pre-claim hook: %v (skipping %s)\n", hookErr, candidate.ID)
360					}
361					emit("preclaim.skipped", map[string]any{
362						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
363						"reason":  hookErr.Error(),
364					})
365					continue
366				}
367			}
368	
369			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
370				continue
371			}
372	
373			emit("bead.claimed", map[string]any{
374				"bead_id":  candidate.ID,
375				"title":    candidate.Title,
376				"assignee": assignee,
377			})
378	
379			if opts.Log != nil {
380				if candidate.Title != "" {
381					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "\n▶ %s: %s\n", candidate.ID, candidate.Title)
382				} else {
383					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "\n▶ %s\n", candidate.ID)
384				}
385			}
386	
387			// Generate a provisional attempt_id for progress events.
388			// The real attempt_id is assigned inside ExecuteBead; we use this
389			// for queueing/running events and replace with the real one once known.
390			provAttemptID := time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomProgressID()
391			runStart := now()
392			phaseSeq := 0
393			nextPhase := func(phase string, heartbeat bool) {
394				phaseSeq++
395				emitProgress(opts.ProgressCh, newProgressEvent(
396					opts.WorkerID, opts.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, provAttemptID,
397					phase, phaseSeq, heartbeat, now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(), opts,
398				))
399			}
400	
401			nextPhase("queueing", false)
402	
403			hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
404			var hbWG sync.WaitGroup
405			hbWG.Add(1)
406			go func(beadID string) {
407				defer hbWG.Done()
408				ticker := time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval)
409				defer ticker.Stop()
410				for {
411					select {
412					case <-hbCtx.Done():
413						return
414					case <-ticker.C:
415						_ = w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)
416					}
417				}
418			}(candidate.ID)
419	
420			nextPhase("running", false)
421	
422			report, err := w.Executor.Execute(ctx, candidate.ID)
423			hbCancel()
424			hbWG.Wait()
425			if err != nil {
426				report = ExecuteBeadReport{
427					BeadID: candidate.ID,
428					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
429					Detail: err.Error(),
430				}
431			}
432			if report.BeadID == "" {
433				report.BeadID = candidate.ID
434			}
435			if report.Status == "" {
436				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
437			}
438			if report.Detail == "" {
439				report.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(report.Status, "", "")
440			}
441	
442			result.Attempts++
443	
444			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
445				appendLoopRoutingEvidence(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, now().UTC())
446				// Close the bead early when review is skipped. The closure gate
447				// (ddx-e30e60a9) accepts this path because closing_commit_sha is
448				// set and there is no malformed-APPROVE event to reject.
449				reviewApproved := true
450				reviewSkipped := w.Reviewer == nil || opts.NoReview || HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip")
451	
452				if reviewSkipped {
453					if err := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
454						return result, err
455					}
456				}
457	
458				if w.Reviewer != nil && !opts.NoReview && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip") {
459					reviewRes, reviewErr := w.Reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev, report.Harness, report.Model)
460					if reviewErr != nil {
461						// Review error: log event but leave bead closed (don't block on reviewer failures).
462						_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
463							Kind:      "review-error",
464							Summary:   "review agent error",
465							Body:      reviewErr.Error(),
466							Actor:     assignee,
467							Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
468							CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
469						})
470					} else {
471						report.ReviewVerdict = string(reviewRes.Verdict)
472						report.ReviewRationale = reviewRes.Rationale
473						// Persist the full reviewer stream as an artifact so the
474						// event body never carries the raw stream (ddx-f8a11202).
475						// On error the artifact path is empty and the event body
476						// still contains the short verdict summary; callers of this
477						// loop can recover the full text from the reviewer session
478						// log if the artifact write failed.
479						artifactPath, artifactErr := persistReviewerStream(opts.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, report.AttemptID, reviewRes.RawOutput)
480						if artifactErr != nil && opts.Log != nil {
481							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "reviewer stream artifact: %v\n", artifactErr)
482						}
483	
484						switch reviewRes.Verdict {
485						case VerdictApprove:
486							// Approved: record the verdict event and then close.
487							// Closure must land AFTER the review event so the
488							// gate (ddx-e30e60a9) sees the terminal verdict.
489							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
490								Kind:      "review",
491								Summary:   "APPROVE",
492								Body:      reviewEventBody("APPROVE", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath),
493								Actor:     assignee,
494								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
495								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
496							})
497							if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); cerr != nil {
498								return result, cerr
499							}
500						case VerdictRequestChanges:
501							// Needs fixes: record the review verdict, then reopen
502							// with findings in notes. The review event must land
503							// even on non-approve paths so review-outcomes can
504							// attribute the rejection to the originating tier.
505							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
506								Kind:      "review",
507								Summary:   "REQUEST_CHANGES",
508								Body:      reviewEventBody("REQUEST_CHANGES", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath),
509								Actor:     assignee,
510								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
511								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
512							})
513							reopenNotes := reviewRes.Rationale
514							if reopenNotes == "" {
515								reopenNotes = reviewRes.RawOutput
516							}
517							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: REQUEST_CHANGES", reopenNotes); reopenErr != nil {
518								return result, reopenErr
519							}
520							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
521							report.Detail = "post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
522							reviewApproved = false
523						case VerdictBlock:
524							rationale := strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale)
525							if rationale == "" {
526								_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
527									Kind:      "review-malfunction",
528									Summary:   "BLOCK without rationale",
529									Body:      reviewEventBody("BLOCK without rationale", "", artifactPath),
530									Actor:     assignee,
531									Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
532									CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
533								})
534								report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
535								report.Detail = "post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)"
536								report.ReviewRationale = ""
537								reviewApproved = false
538								break
539							}
540							// Cannot proceed: record the verdict, then reopen and
541							// flag for human with BLOCK marker plus actionable rationale.
542							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
543								Kind:      "review",
544								Summary:   "BLOCK",
545								Body:      rationale,
546								Actor:     assignee,
547								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
548								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
549							})
550							blockNotes := "REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n" + rationale
551							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: BLOCK", blockNotes); reopenErr != nil {
552								return result, reopenErr
553							}
554							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock
555							report.Detail = "post-merge review: BLOCK (flagged for human)"
556							reviewApproved = false
557						}
558					}
559				}
560	
561				if reviewApproved {
562					result.Successes++
563					result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
564				} else {
565					result.Failures++
566					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
567				}
568			} else {
569				if err := w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID); err != nil {
570					return result, err
571				}
572				if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges {
573					count, cerr := w.Store.IncrNoChangesCount(candidate.ID)
574					if cerr != nil {
575						return result, cerr
576					}
577					satisfied, evidence, aerr := w.adjudicateNoChanges(ctx, candidate.ID, count, maxNoChangesBeforeClose, report.NoChangesRationale)
578					if aerr != nil {
579						return result, aerr
580					}
581					if satisfied {
582						// Adjudication confirmed bead is already satisfied.
583						// Set the terminal status BEFORE the close so the late
584						// executeBeadLoopEvent append captures "already_satisfied"
585						// (not "no_changes"), and emit an early execute-bead
586						// evidence event so the closure gate accepts even when
587						// BaseRev is empty (test fixtures and genuinely-no-commit
588						// satisfied beads).
589						report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied
590						if evidence != "" {
591							// Checker evidence explains why the bead is being closed;
592							// it takes precedence over the executor's attempt detail.
593							report.Detail = evidence
594						}
595						_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC()))
596						if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.BaseRev); cerr != nil {
597							return result, cerr
598						}
599						result.Successes++
600						result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
601					} else {
602						// Unresolved: suppress immediate retry so the queue can
603						// move on to other beads.
604						if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
605							retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(noProgressCooldown)
606							if cerr := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); cerr != nil {
607								return result, cerr
608							}
609							report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
610						}
611						result.Failures++
612						result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
613					}
614				} else {
615					if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
616						retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(noProgressCooldown)
617						if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
618							return result, err
619						}
620						report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
621					}
622					result.Failures++
623					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
624				}
625			}
626	
627			result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
628	
629			// Skip the late execute-bead append for already-satisfied beads —
630			// the satisfied path appends its own terminal event before
631			// CloseWithEvidence so the closure gate sees execution evidence.
632			// Duplicating it here would yield two identical events.
633			if report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
634				if err := w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC())); err != nil {
635					return result, err
636				}
637			}
638	
639			// Emit terminal progress phase event.
640			terminalPhase := "failed"
641			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess || report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
642				terminalPhase = "done"
643			} else if report.PreserveRef != "" {
644				terminalPhase = "preserved"
645			}
646			// Use the real attempt_id from the report if available.
647			finalAttemptID := report.AttemptID
648			if finalAttemptID == "" {
649				finalAttemptID = provAttemptID
650			}
651			phaseSeq++
652			emitProgress(opts.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
653				EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
654				AttemptID: finalAttemptID,
655				WorkerID:  opts.WorkerID,
656				ProjectID: opts.ProjectRoot,
657				BeadID:    candidate.ID,
658				Harness:   opts.Harness,
659				Model:     opts.Model,
660				Profile:   opts.Profile,
661				Phase:     terminalPhase,
662				PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
663				Heartbeat: false,
664				TS:        now().UTC(),
665				ElapsedMS: now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
666				Message:   report.Detail,
667			})
668	
669			emit("bead.result", map[string]any{
670				"bead_id":              candidate.ID,
671				"status":               report.Status,
672				"detail":               report.Detail,
673				"session_id":           report.SessionID,
674				"result_rev":           report.ResultRev,
675				"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
676				"preserve_ref":         report.PreserveRef,
677				"no_changes_rationale": report.NoChangesRationale,
678				"duration_ms":          now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
679			})
680	
681			if opts.Log != nil {
682				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "✓ %s → %s\n", candidate.ID, formatLoopResult(report))
683			}
684	
685			if opts.Once {
686				return result, nil
687			}
688		}
689	}

691	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) nextCandidate(attempted map[string]struct{}, labelFilter string) (bead.Bead, bool, error) {
692		ready, err := w.Store.ReadyExecution()
693		if err != nil {
694			return bead.Bead{}, false, err
695		}
696		for _, candidate := range ready {
697			if _, seen := attempted[candidate.ID]; seen {
698				continue
699			}
700			if labelFilter != "" && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, labelFilter) {
701				continue
702			}
703			return candidate, true, nil
704		}
705		return bead.Bead{}, false, nil
706	}

708	// appendLoopRoutingEvidence records a kind:routing evidence event on the bead
709	// from the executor's ExecuteBeadReport, so that review-outcomes analytics can
710	// attribute a subsequent review verdict to the originating provider/model tier.
711	// Best-effort: errors and missing-provider cases are silently ignored.
712	func appendLoopRoutingEvidence(store BeadEventAppender, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport, createdAt time.Time) {
713		if store == nil || beadID == "" {
714			return
715		}
716		provider := report.Provider
717		if provider == "" {
718			provider = report.Harness
719		}
720		if provider == "" {
721			return
722		}
723		body, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
724			"resolved_provider": provider,
725			"resolved_model":    report.Model,
726			"fallback_chain":    []string{},
727			"requested_profile": report.RequestedProfile,
728			"requested_tier":    report.RequestedTier,
729			"resolved_tier":     report.ResolvedTier,
730			"escalation_count":  report.EscalationCount,
731			"final_tier":        report.FinalTier,
732		})
733		if err != nil {
734			return
735		}
736		summary := "provider=" + provider
737		if report.Model != "" {
738			summary += " model=" + report.Model
739		}
740		_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
741			Kind:      "routing",
742			Summary:   summary,
743			Body:      string(body),
744			Actor:     "ddx",
745			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
746			CreatedAt: createdAt,
747		})
748	}

750	func executeBeadLoopEvent(report ExecuteBeadReport, actor string, createdAt time.Time) bead.BeadEvent {
751		parts := []string{}
752		if report.Detail != "" {
753			parts = append(parts, report.Detail)
754		}
755		if report.Tier != "" {
756			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s", report.Tier))
757		}
758		if report.ProbeResult != "" {
759			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("probe_result=%s", report.ProbeResult))
760		}
761		if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
762			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("rationale: %s", report.NoChangesRationale))
763		}
764		if report.ReviewRationale != "" {
765			parts = append(parts, report.ReviewRationale)
766		}
767		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
768			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("preserve_ref=%s", report.PreserveRef))
769		}
770		if report.ResultRev != "" {
771			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("result_rev=%s", report.ResultRev))
772		}
773		if report.BaseRev != "" {
774			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("base_rev=%s", report.BaseRev))
775		}
776		if report.RetryAfter != "" {
777			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("retry_after=%s", report.RetryAfter))
778		}
779	
780		return bead.BeadEvent{
781			Kind:      "execute-bead",
782			Summary:   report.Status,
783			Body:      strings.Join(parts, "\n"),
784			Actor:     actor,
785			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
786			CreatedAt: createdAt,
787		}
788	}

790	// writeLoopEvent emits one structured JSONL line to sink describing a
791	// milestone in an execute-bead loop run. Entries use the same envelope as
792	// the ddx-agent harness (session_id/seq/type/ts/data) so existing log
793	// aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, ddx server workers log) can parse
794	// the stream uniformly. Errors are swallowed: structured logging must
795	// never break the core execute-loop.
796	func writeLoopEvent(sink io.Writer, sessionID, eventType string, data map[string]any, ts time.Time) {
797		if sink == nil {
798			return
799		}
800		if data == nil {
801			data = map[string]any{}
802		}
803		entry := map[string]any{
804			"session_id": sessionID,
805			"type":       eventType,
806			"ts":         ts.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
807			"data":       data,
808		}
809		line, err := json.Marshal(entry)
810		if err != nil {
811			return
812		}
813		_, _ = sink.Write(line)
814		_, _ = sink.Write([]byte("\n"))
815	}

817	// formatLoopResult returns a concise human-readable summary of a bead execution
818	// result using merged/preserved/error terminology instead of raw status codes.
819	func formatLoopResult(report ExecuteBeadReport) string {
820		switch report.Status {
821		case ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
822			shortRev := report.ResultRev
823			if len(shortRev) > 8 {
824				shortRev = shortRev[:8]
825			}
826			if shortRev != "" {
827				return fmt.Sprintf("merged (%s)", shortRev)
828			}
829			return "merged"
830		case ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
831			return "already_satisfied"
832		case ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges:
833			if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
834				return fmt.Sprintf("no_changes: %s", report.NoChangesRationale)
835			}
836			return "no_changes"
837		default:
838			detail := report.Detail
839			if detail == "" {
840				detail = report.Status
841			}
842			if report.PreserveRef != "" {
843				return fmt.Sprintf("preserved: %s", detail)
844			}
845			return fmt.Sprintf("error: %s", detail)
846		}
847	}
848	
849	// reCommitSHA matches a 7-to-40 character lowercase hex string that looks like a
850	// git commit SHA. Used to detect whether a no_changes rationale cites a prior commit.
851	var reCommitSHA = regexp.MustCompile(`\b[0-9a-f]{7,40}\b`)
852	
853	// reTestFuncName matches a Go test function name (TestXxx or BenchmarkXxx).
854	var reTestFuncName = regexp.MustCompile(`\b(?:Test|Benchmark)[A-Z]\w*\b`)
855	
856	// rationaleIsSpecific returns true when the rationale string contains a reference
857	// specific enough to treat a no_changes outcome as already_satisfied on the first
858	// attempt. Currently this means: the rationale cites a commit SHA (7+ hex chars)
859	// or a Go test function name. Vague rationales ("nothing to do") return false.
860	func rationaleIsSpecific(rationale string) bool {
861		if rationale == "" {
862			return false
863		}
864		return reCommitSHA.MatchString(rationale) || reTestFuncName.MatchString(rationale)
865	}
866	
867	// adjudicateNoChanges runs the no-change adjudication step for a bead.
868	// It returns (satisfied, evidence, err). When satisfied is true the bead
869	// should be closed as already_satisfied with the evidence string. When false
870	// retry suppression (cooldown) should be applied and the bead left open.
871	//
872	// If a SatisfactionChecker is configured it is called first. Otherwise:
873	//   - When the report carries a specific rationale (cites a commit SHA or test
874	//     name), the bead is closed as already_satisfied on the first occurrence.
875	//   - Otherwise the default count-based rule applies (close after maxNoChangesBeforeClose).
876	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) adjudicateNoChanges(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount, maxNoChangesBeforeClose int, rationale string) (bool, string, error) {
877		if w.SatisfactionChecker != nil {
878			return w.SatisfactionChecker.CheckSatisfied(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
879		}
880		if rationaleIsSpecific(rationale) {
881			evidence := rationale
882			return true, evidence, nil
883		}
884		if noChangesCount >= maxNoChangesBeforeClose {
885			return true, fmt.Sprintf("no_changes on %d consecutive attempt(s); bead treated as already satisfied", noChangesCount), nil
886		}
887		return false, "", nil
888	}
889	
890	func shouldSuppressNoProgress(report ExecuteBeadReport) bool {
891		if report.BaseRev == "" || report.ResultRev == "" {
892			return false
893		}
894		return report.BaseRev == report.ResultRev
895	}

906		case <-timer.C:
907			return nil
908		}

321	// EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs returns git pathspec-exclusion fragments
322	// applied when landEvidence / VerifyCleanWorktree stage the evidence
323	// directory. Only excludes the multi-thousand-line embedded session log
324	// (the single file that actually explodes past provider context
325	// limits). prompt.md, usage.json, manifest.json, and result.json remain
326	// tracked — they are small and serve the audit-trail contract. The
327	// gitignore written by `ddx init` already treats .ddx/executions/*/
328	// embedded as ignored; this pathspec is defence-in-depth for force-add
329	// paths.
330	//
331	// Regression anchor: ddx-39e27896 — session logs landed as tracked
332	// evidence caused retry prompts to balloon past 2M+ tokens and crash
333	// every provider with n_keep > n_ctx.
334	func EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs() []string {
335		return []string{
336			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/embedded/**",
337		}
338	}
339	
340	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs returns git pathspec-exclusion fragments
341	// applied when review-prompt synthesis runs `git show <rev>` over the
342	// evidence commit. Broader than EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs: excludes
343	// prompt.md and usage.json too, because even though they're tracked for
344	// audit, they're execution-artifact noise from the reviewer's
345	// perspective — the reviewer wants to see the implementation diff, not
346	// the prior attempt's prompt or token counters. This also protects
347	// against old commits (pre-fix) that committed the session log
348	// directly.
349	//
350	// Regression anchor: ddx-39e27896.
351	func EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs() []string {
352		return []string{
353			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/embedded/**",
354			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/prompt.md",
355			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/usage.json",
356		}
357	}

359	// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes, explicitly excluding harness noise
360	// paths, and creates a commit with msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil)
361	// when a commit was made, (false, nil) when nothing real remained to commit
362	// after exclusions, and (false, err) on failure.
363	func (r *RealGitOps) SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error) {
364		// Do NOT list already-gitignored paths (.ddx/agent-logs, .ddx/workers) as
365		// :(exclude) pathspecs. Git treats a path named by :(exclude) as explicitly
366		// referenced, so when the path is also .gitignored git emits "The following
367		// paths are ignored by one of your .gitignore files" AND exits 1 — even
368		// though the pathspec is trying to SKIP it. Paths already in .gitignore are
369		// excluded by default; excludes here are only for paths that would
370		// otherwise be tracked.
371		addArgs := []string{
372			"add", "-A", "--",
373			".",
374		}
375		addArgs = append(addArgs, synthesizeCommitExcludePathspecs(dir)...)
376		if err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, addArgs...).Run(); err != nil {
377			return false, fmt.Errorf("staging changes: %w", err)
378		}
379		statusOut, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--cached", "--name-only").Output()

69	// TestParseReviewVerdictStrict covers ddx-f7ae036f AC #3 and #4: the strict
70	// variant returns a typed error on unparseable input instead of silently
71	// collapsing to BLOCK. Callers that propagate the error get review-error
72	// (retryable) event-loop handling; callers that swallow it get the same
73	// legacy BLOCK default. The difference is the caller's choice, not a silent
74	// mis-record.
75	func TestParseReviewVerdictStrict(t *testing.T) {
76		tests := []struct {
77			name    string
78			input   string
79			want    ReviewVerdict
80			wantErr bool
81		}{
82			{
83				name:    "clean APPROVE",
84				input:   "some preamble\n### Verdict: APPROVE\n\ntrailing",
85				want:    VerdictApprove,
86				wantErr: false,
87			},
88			{
89				name:    "clean BLOCK",
90				input:   "### Verdict: BLOCK\n",
91				want:    VerdictBlock,
92				wantErr: false,
93			},
94			{
95				name:    "clean REQUEST_CHANGES",
96				input:   "### Verdict: REQUEST_CHANGES\n",
97				want:    VerdictRequestChanges,
98				wantErr: false,
99			},
100			{
101				name:    "empty output",
102				input:   "",
103				wantErr: true,
104			},
105			{
106				name:    "unparseable — no verdict line",
107				input:   "Reviewer crashed mid-stream.\nNo structured verdict anywhere.",
108				wantErr: true,
109			},
110			{
111				name:    "unparseable — lowercase inline verdict without heading",
112				input:   "I would say verdict: APPROVE but I haven't formatted it",
113				wantErr: true,
114			},
115		}
116		for _, tc := range tests {
117			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
118				got, err := ParseReviewVerdictStrict(tc.input)
119				if tc.wantErr {
120					assert.ErrorIs(t, err, ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable,
121						"unparseable input must surface a typed error — the silent default-to-BLOCK behavior is what caused the f7ae036f incident")
122					return
123				}
124				assert.NoError(t, err)
125				assert.Equal(t, tc.want, got)
126			})
127		}

495	// TestExecuteBeadWorkerReviewBoundedByMaxTier verifies that REQUEST_CHANGES
496	// reopens the bead for the next attempt but does not cause an infinite loop
497	// within a single worker.Run call. The bead is visited once, the reviewer
498	// returns REQUEST_CHANGES, the bead is reopened — but the "attempted" map
499	// prevents it from being picked up again in the same run.
500	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerReviewBoundedByMaxTier(t *testing.T) {
501		// Use a single-bead store so we can assert "exactly one attempt".
502		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
503		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
504		only := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-bound-1", Title: "Impossible AC"}
505		require.NoError(t, store.Create(only))
506	
507		executorCalls := 0
508		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
509			Store: store,
510			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
511				executorCalls++
512				return ExecuteBeadReport{
513					BeadID:    beadID,
514					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
515					SessionID: "sess-bound",
516					ResultRev: "11111111",
517				}, nil
518			}),
519			Reviewer: makeReviewer(VerdictRequestChanges, "### Verdict: REQUEST_CHANGES\n\nStill failing."),
520		}
521	
522		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{
523			Assignee: "worker",
524			// no Once flag: drain the queue fully within this run
525		})
526		require.NoError(t, err)
527	
528		// The executor should have been called exactly once; the attempted map
529		// prevents revisiting even after the bead is reopened by the reviewer.
530		assert.Equal(t, 1, executorCalls, "bead should be attempted exactly once within a single worker.Run call")
531		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Successes)
532		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Failures, "reopened bead counts as a failure in this run")
533	
534		// Bead is open again — available for the next worker.Run call.
535		got, err := store.Get(only.ID)
536		require.NoError(t, err)
537		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status)
538	}

565	// TestGitShowExcludesEvidenceNoiseFromReviewDiff is the regression for
566	// ddx-39e27896. A prior attempt that tracked a multi-thousand-line
567	// session log (.ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/agent-*.jsonl) in git
568	// history would cause DefaultBeadReviewer.gitShow to emit a <diff>
569	// section sized O(session log), pushing retry prompts past 2M tokens
570	// and crashing every provider with n_keep > n_ctx.
571	//
572	// This test creates a synthetic repo matching the failure scenario —
573	// a commit that adds a 10k-line embedded session log — then runs
574	// gitShow and asserts the output excludes the embedded file content
575	// and stays bounded.
576	func TestGitShowExcludesEvidenceNoiseFromReviewDiff(t *testing.T) {
577		root := t.TempDir()
578		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
579		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
580		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
581	
582		// Seed commit so we have a base rev.
583		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, "README.md"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
584			t.Fatalf("write seed: %v", err)
585		}
586		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "README.md")
587		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "seed")
588	
589		// Synthetic evidence commit that adds a multi-thousand-line session log
590		// PLUS a legitimate implementation change. The fix must exclude the
591		// session log from the diff while keeping the implementation change.
592		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx", "executions", "20260417T000000-testattempt", "embedded")
593		if err := os.MkdirAll(evidenceDir, 0o755); err != nil {
594			t.Fatalf("mkdir evidence: %v", err)
595		}
596		var bigLog strings.Builder
597		for i := 0; i < 10000; i++ {
598			fmt.Fprintf(&bigLog, "{\"seq\":%d,\"event\":\"tool_call\",\"payload\":\"lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur adipiscing elit\"}\n", i)
599		}
600		sessionLogPath := filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "agent-123.jsonl")
601		if err := os.WriteFile(sessionLogPath, []byte(bigLog.String()), 0o644); err != nil {
602			t.Fatalf("write session log: %v", err)
603		}
604	
605		// Legitimate implementation change that MUST survive in the diff.
606		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, "implementation.go"), []byte("package main\n\nfunc Added() {}\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
607			t.Fatalf("write implementation: %v", err)
608		}
609	
610		// Force-add the evidence (the pre-fix landEvidence behavior) plus the real change.
611		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "-f", ".ddx/executions/")
612		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "implementation.go")
613		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: add execution evidence [testattempt] + impl")
614	
615		// Get the HEAD sha (the evidence commit).
616		headSha := strings.TrimSpace(runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD"))
617	
618		// Call the gitShow method with the fix in place.
619		reviewer := &DefaultBeadReviewer{ProjectRoot: root}
620		out, err := reviewer.gitShow(headSha)
621		if err != nil {
622			t.Fatalf("gitShow: %v", err)
623		}
624	
625		// Must NOT include the session log content.
626		if strings.Contains(out, "lorem ipsum dolor sit amet") {
627			t.Errorf("gitShow output includes embedded session log content (pathspec exclusion not applied)")
628		}
629	
630		// Must include the legitimate implementation change.
631		if !strings.Contains(out, "implementation.go") {
632			t.Errorf("gitShow output missing the legitimate implementation file (pathspec too aggressive)")
633		}
634		if !strings.Contains(out, "func Added()") {
635			t.Errorf("gitShow output missing the implementation change body")
636		}
637	
638		// Must be bounded in size. The raw session log was ~1MB; fixed diff
639		// should be under 50KB easily (seed + impl.go + evidence metadata).
640		if len(out) > 150_000 {
641			t.Errorf("gitShow output size %d exceeds 150KB budget — pathspec exclusion did not bound the diff", len(out))
642		}
643	}

14	// AC #3: on any reviewer terminal failure — nonzero exit, empty output, or
15	// unparseable output — the loop must record a failure event and leave the
16	// bead un-closed so a later attempt can retry. Closing on reviewer failure
17	// is the silent-false-closure surface that 738edf47 eliminates.
18	func TestExecuteBeadWorker_ReviewerFailureModesKeepBeadOpen(t *testing.T) {

22		}{

26					return nil, errors.New("reviewer harness exited with code 1")
27				}),
28			},
29			{
30				name: "reviewer output empty — parse returns unparseable",
31				reviewer: BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
32					return &ReviewResult{
33						Verdict:   "",
34						RawOutput: "",
35					}, nil
36				}),
37			},
38			{
39				name: "reviewer output unparseable — no recognizable verdict line",
40				reviewer: BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
41					return &ReviewResult{
42						Verdict:   "",
43						RawOutput: "Reviewer crashed mid-stream with no structured verdict.",
44					}, nil
45				}),
46			},
47		}
48		for _, tc := range tests {

57							SessionID: "sess-x",
58							ResultRev: "c0ffee" + tc.name[:4],
59						}, nil
60					}),

62				}

64				_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{Assignee: "worker", Once: true})

15	func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadReviewCommand() *cobra.Command {
16		cmd := &cobra.Command{
17			Use:   "review <id>",
18			Short: "Generate a review prompt for a bead's implementation",
19			Long: `Generate a review-ready prompt for a bead implementation.
20	
21	The prompt includes:
22	  - Bead title, description, and acceptance criteria
23	  - Full content of governing documents (spec-id refs from the bead)
24	  - Git diff of the reviewed commit (git show)
25	  - Review instructions with the expected APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK output contract
26	
27	By default the commit is taken from the bead's closing_commit_sha field.
28	Use --from-rev to override.
29	
30	Pipe the output into ddx agent run:
31	
32	  ddx bead review <id> | ddx agent run --prompt @-
33	  ddx bead review <id> --output /tmp/review.md && ddx agent run --prompt /tmp/review.md`,
34			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
35			RunE: f.runBeadReview,
36		}
37		cmd.Flags().String("from-rev", "", "Commit SHA to review (default: closing_commit_sha from bead)")
38		cmd.Flags().Int("iter", 1, "Review iteration number (shown in prompt header and grade table)")
39		cmd.Flags().String("output", "", "Write prompt to file instead of stdout")
40		return cmd
41	}
42	
43	func (f *CommandFactory) runBeadReview(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
44		beadID := args[0]
45		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from-rev")
46		iter, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("iter")
47		outputFile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("output")
48	
49		s := f.beadStore()
50		b, err := s.Get(beadID)
51		if err != nil {
52			return err
53		}
54	
55		// Resolve the commit SHA to review.
56		// Priority: --from-rev flag > closing_commit_sha on the bead.
57		rev := strings.TrimSpace(fromRev)
58		if rev == "" {
59			if sha, ok := b.Extra["closing_commit_sha"].(string); ok {
60				rev = strings.TrimSpace(sha)
61			}
62		}
63		if rev == "" {
64			return fmt.Errorf("no commit to review: use --from-rev <sha>, or close the bead with a commit SHA first (ddx bead close --commit <sha>)")
65		}
66	
67		// Project root is used for git operations and governing doc file reads.
68		projectRoot := gitpkg.FindProjectRoot(f.WorkingDir)
69		if projectRoot == "" {
70			projectRoot = f.WorkingDir
71		}
72	
73		// Fetch the git diff for the commit.
74		diff, err := beadReviewGitShow(projectRoot, rev)
75		if err != nil {
76			return fmt.Errorf("git show %s: %w", rev, err)
77		}
78	
79		// Resolve governing document references from the bead's spec-id field.
80		refs := agent.ResolveGoverningRefs(projectRoot, b)
81	
82		// Delegate prompt assembly to the single source of truth in the agent
83		// package so the CLI handler and the post-merge reviewer cannot drift.
84		prompt := agent.BuildReviewPrompt(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs)
85	
86		if outputFile != "" {
87			return os.WriteFile(outputFile, []byte(prompt), 0o644)
88		}
89		_, err = fmt.Fprint(cmd.OutOrStdout(), prompt)
90		return err
91	}
92	
93	// beadReviewGitShow runs `git show <rev>` with pathspec exclusions for
94	// execution-evidence noise so the review prompt's <diff> section stays
95	// bounded. See agent.EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs and ddx-39e27896.
96	func beadReviewGitShow(projectRoot, rev string) (string, error) {
97		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
98		defer cancel()
99		args := append([]string{"show", rev, "--", "."}, agent.EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
100		out, err := gitpkg.Command(ctx, projectRoot, args...).Output()
101		if err != nil {
102			return "", err
103		}
104		return string(out), nil
105	}

Committed. Stage C1 of FEAT-022 done.

AC walkthrough:
- `TestBuildReviewPrompt_*` (governing-doc clamp, diff clamp, floor preservation under 20× pressure, ranked inclusion, large-file degradation, pre-dispatch short-circuit) — all in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_evidence_test.go`.
- `TestReviewContextOverflow` — drives `DefaultBeadReviewer` with a `countingRunner` and asserts `calls == 0` plus a `review-error` event body containing `context_overflow`.
- `TestReviewFailureNotCloseBead` — wraps the existing `TestExecuteBeadWorker_ReviewerFailureModesKeepBeadOpen` to expose the named anchor; the underlying file remains untouched.
- `TestEvidencePrimitiveUsage` — AST-walks `execute_bead_review.go`, asserts `internal/evidence` import and that no `os.ReadFile`/`exec.Command("git","show",...)` survives without a `//evidence:allow-unbounded` directive (one such directive added above the artifact-reader's `os.ReadFile`).
